brands.name: KeyGlee

riskFlags.title: Severe unit contraction
riskFlags.detail: System fell from 111 total units (106 franchised + 5 company-owned, 2023 FDD) to 59 units per Entrepreneur's 2026 listing, which shows a -21% one-year growth rate. Roughly half the system has exited in ~2 years.

riskFlags.title: Regulatory risk to the wholesaling business model
riskFlags.detail: KeyGlee's core model is real estate wholesaling (assignment of purchase contracts). States are tightening rules: Illinois treats more than one wholesale deal per 12 months as unlicensed brokerage; Kentucky HB 62 (2023) heavily restricts wholesale marketing; Nebraska (2024) requires a real estate license to publicly market an equitable interest. Franchisees in restrictive states may need licenses or face enforcement. No KeyGlee-specific lawsuit was found in this research, but the model itself is under scrutiny.
riskFlags.source: REsimpli wholesaling laws by state guide (and KY/NE statutes referenced therein)

riskFlags.title: High and unusual fee structure
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur lists a dual royalty of 9%/18% and a franchise fee range of $45,000-$100,000 (2023 FDD listed $100,000 flat). The 18% tier and $250/mo technology fee are well above home-services norms; conflicting figures across sources warrant direct FDD verification.

totalInvestmentMin.note: CONFLICT: 2023 FDD-based sources (franchisepayback.com) list $122,000 min; Entrepreneur's current listing shows $67,300

royaltyPercent.note: Entrepreneur lists dual structure 9%/18%; 18% tier likely tied to lower upfront fee option

avgUnitRevenue.note: Franchisepayback shows a $253,000 average revenue figure but notes the franchisor does not formally disclose it; not treated as a verified Item 19 number

item19Available.note: Per franchisepayback, franchisor does not formally disclose financial performance

fddAvailable.note: 2023 FDD on FDD Exchange; no newer public FDD located in this research

exclusiveTerritories.note: CONFLICT: marketing-oriented sources claim exclusive geographic protection; Entrepreneur FDD-derived data says no exclusive territories

ownerTypes.note: Absentee not allowed, part-time not allowed

trainingWeeks.note: 40 hours on-the-job + 43 hours classroom (~2 weeks); franchisepayback cites 30 hours initial plus up to 48 hours on-the-job support

value: national ad fund (up to 1% of gross sales)

minFootprint.note: Home-based permitted; no minimum footprint disclosed

totalUnits.note: Down from 111 total (106 franchised + 5 company) in 2023 FDD

closureCount.note: Exact Item 20 closure counts not obtained, but net decline of ~50 units between 2023 FDD and 2026 listing implies substantial closures/exits

isGrowing.note: -21% unit growth per Entrepreneur; still advertising open franchise opportunities

brands.notes: Real estate wholesaling franchise (Tempe/Phoenix AZ origin). Still actively offering franchises per Entrepreneur, but the system is shrinking sharply and the wholesaling model faces growing state-level licensing restrictions. Data conflicts across sources (founding year, fee, investment range) suggest the FDD has changed materially between 2023 and the current listing; verify against the latest FDD before publishing.

brands.name: Image One Facility Solutions

riskFlags.title: Conflicting fee and investment figures across sources
riskFlags.detail: Franchise fee reported as $24,750-$29,750 (older sources/thefranchisemall), 'up to $29,750' (franchimp 2021 FDD data), and $39,750 (Vetted Biz, 2025). Total investment reported variously as $37,360-$103,208, $43,025-$91,375, and $49,900-$72,049. Figures have evidently risen across FDD years; verify against the current FDD.

riskFlags.title: Small system
riskFlags.detail: Roughly 20-23 franchised locations after ~14 years of franchising; slow but positive growth (10 units in 2021 FDD to ~23 in 2025).

totalInvestmentMin.note: CONFLICT: other sources list $37,360 or $43,025 minimums (older FDD years)

totalInvestmentMax.note: CONFLICT: other sources list maxima up to $103,208

adFundPercent.note: Marketing fee of 2% of monthly sales

avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19 average yearly gross sales; Vetted Biz estimates owner-operator earnings $39,266-$49,082

item19Available.note: FDD includes Item 19 with annual gross revenue data

exclusiveTerritories.note: Territory terms not disclosed in publicly reviewed material

ownerTypes.note: Vetted Biz earnings model assumes owner-operator; other ownership modes unverified

trainingWeeks.note: Five days at corporate HQ in Clearwater, FL, plus ongoing training

value: brand marketing programs funded by 2% marketing fee

totalUnits.note: CONFLICT: other 2025 sources report 20 franchised units, 0 company-owned

yearFounded.note: Image One Facility Solutions, Inc. incorporated in Illinois Nov 3, 2010; predecessor Image One brand history dates earlier (one source says franchising since 2001 under prior ownership)

yearFranchising.note: CONFLICT: franchimp (FDD-derived) says affiliate franchises offered starting 2014; thefranchisemall says 2011

isGrowing.note: 10 franchised units in 2021 FDD to ~20-23 in 2025; slow but positive

brands.notes: B2B commercial/janitorial cleaning franchise out of Rolling Meadows, IL (training in Clearwater, FL). Transparent FDD with Item 19. Main data quality issue is the spread of fee/investment figures across FDD years; the 2025 Vetted Biz figures were used as most current.

brands.name: GoldenTrust Insurance

riskFlags.title: Insurance licensing required
riskFlags.detail: Operating an independent insurance agency requires state insurance producer licensing (franchisor's 40-hour training includes licensing assistance, implying owners must obtain a license). Adds a regulatory gate and ramp time before revenue.

riskFlags.title: Territory exclusivity claims conflict with FDD-derived data
riskFlags.detail: Franchisor marketing says 'Your territory is yours. No internal competition,' but Entrepreneur's FDD-derived listing reports NO exclusive territories. Verify Item 12 directly.

riskFlags.title: Geographic concentration in Florida/Texas
riskFlags.detail: System built almost entirely in Florida (HQ Doral, FL) with early Texas expansion. Heavy exposure to the volatile Florida property insurance market.

adFundPercent.note: Ad fee is a flat $1,000/month, not a percentage; also $125/user/month technology fee

item19Available.note: No Item 19 data found in any reviewed source; franchisor site makes no earnings claims

fddAvailable.note: Franchising since 2021 with FDD-derived data in Entrepreneur directory; FDD itself not located publicly

exclusiveTerritories.note: CONFLICT: franchisor marketing claims exclusive territory protection

value: corporate marketing programs funded by $1,000/month ad fee

totalUnits.note: CONFLICT: franchisor site claims 75+ active franchises (2026); Entrepreneur FDD-derived count is 56

isGrowing.note: +273% growth rate per Entrepreneur; 0 to ~56-75 agencies since 2021

brands.notes: Independent insurance agency franchise, HQ Doral, FL; founded 2015, franchising 2021. One of the cheapest entries in insurance franchising with a low 3% royalty and flat $1,000/mo ad fee, in-house financing, and fast unit growth concentrated in FL/TX. No public Item 19 found. Buyers need state insurance licensing.

brands.name: CPA Moms

riskFlags.title: Stagnant micro-system
riskFlags.detail: Only 7 units with 0.0% growth per Entrepreneur (2026) after franchising since 2020. Very limited validation pool and network effects.

riskFlags.title: Active CPA license required
riskFlags.detail: Franchise applications are only accepted from actively licensed CPAs, drastically narrowing the buyer pool and making the license a hard prerequisite to operate.

riskFlags.title: High fixed technology fee relative to size
riskFlags.detail: $595/month technology fee plus 10% royalty on a small home-based practice can be a meaningful drag in early ramp.

fddAvailable.note: FDD provided to applicants per franchisor site; not located in public repositories

veteranDiscount.note: Listed as not applicable on Entrepreneur

ownerTypes.note: Absentee not allowed; built for licensed-CPA owner-operators (part-time permitted)

yearFounded.note: Founded by Mayumi Young; the CPA MOMS brand/network predates the 2020 franchise entity

brands.notes: Virtual accounting/tax franchise exclusively for licensed CPA mothers; 5-year agreement term (short vs industry norm of 10). Genuine franchise with FDD, but a niche micro-system: 7 flat units, 10% royalty, $595/mo tech fee, no exclusive territories. No Item 19 data found publicly.

brands.name: Dope CFO Certified Advisor

riskFlags.title: Not currently accepting new franchisees
riskFlags.detail: Franchising.com states Dope CFO is not currently accepting new applicants; system sits at 18 units. The franchise channel appears paused while the company's primary business remains its non-franchise training program.

riskFlags.title: Hybrid franchise/certification model — verify what is being purchased
riskFlags.detail: DOPE CFO operates two distinct offerings: a non-franchise cannabis-accounting training/certification program sold via dopecfo.com (the bulk of its 'certified advisor' community), and a separate registered franchise ('Dope CFO Certified Advisor', franchising since June 2, 2022) for fractional-CFO practices. Most advisors using the brand are program graduates, not franchisees.

riskFlags.title: Cannabis-industry dependency
riskFlags.detail: Client base is cannabis/CBD/hemp operators: federal illegality, IRC 280E tax burden, banking friction, and client failure rates create elevated revenue volatility. Cannabis-adjacent service businesses are also generally ineligible for SBA-guaranteed financing.
riskFlags.source: CFO.com profile of Dope CFO / SBA SOP 50 10 eligibility rules

fddAvailable.note: Began offering franchises June 2, 2022; FDD-derived data appears in directories but FDD not located in free public repositories

sbaApproved.note: SBA SOP excludes direct and indirect marijuana-related businesses from SBA-guaranteed lending; a cannabis-focused CFO franchise would generally be ineligible

ownerTypes.note: Absentee not allowed, part-time not allowed; designed for accounting/finance professionals

trainingWeeks.note: 120 hours classroom training (~3 work-weeks) plus on-the-job component

value: client acquisition system (offer letters, price quoting tools, prospect emails)

yearFounded.note: Founded by Andrew Hunzicker, CPA (Bend, OR)

isGrowing.note: 18 units; not accepting new applicants — franchise channel effectively paused

brands.notes: Anomalous model as suspected: the well-known DOPE CFO product is a non-franchise cannabis-accounting certification/training program (dopecfo.com); 'Dope CFO Certified Advisor' is a separate, small registered franchise (since 6/2022, 18 units, $75k fee, 8% royalty, 5-year term) whose new-applicant intake is currently paused per franchising.com. Treat as effectively dormant for new buyers and confirm which offering any listing refers to.

brands.name: Canopy Lawn Care

riskFlags.title: Item 19 based on a single affiliate-owned unit with excluded expenses
riskFlags.detail: The 2025 FDD Item 19 reflects one affiliate unit in Raleigh, NC (opened 2016): $963,529 revenue, 23.1% margin after disclosed expenses — but 'certain expenses have been excluded that a franchise operator may incur,' including facility rent and a full-time manager. No franchisee results are included.

riskFlags.title: Zero franchisee track record before 2024
riskFlags.detail: Item 20 shows 0 franchised units through 2023 and all 37 franchised units opened in 2024. The entire franchise cohort is brand-new; renewal, attrition, and unit economics at franchisee level are untested.

riskFlags.title: Heavy mandatory marketing spend and ramping minimum royalties
riskFlags.detail: Required local advertising spend is the greater of $30,000/year for the first territory (+$10,000 per additional contiguous territory) or 10% of prior-year gross revenues, on top of the 8% royalty whose monthly minimum ramps to $3,375 per territory after 61 months, plus a 1% brand fund (cap 2%).

riskFlags.title: Owned by Empower Brands; brand repositioned
riskFlags.detail: Canopy was acquired by multi-brand platform Empower Brands and refocused exclusively on lawn treatment programs; it did not rank on Entrepreneur's 2026 Franchise 500.

franchiseFee.note: Plus $0.95 per single-family dwelling unit for territories exceeding 45,000 dwellings

royaltyPercent.note: Greater of 8% of gross revenues or minimum royalty ($500-$3,375/month per territory by tenure)

adFundPercent.note: Brand fund currently 1%, contractual max 2%; CONFLICT: franchisepayback lists 4%, likely conflating brand fund with local ad requirements

avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19 (2025 FDD, 2024 data) — single affiliate-owned Raleigh NC unit, NOT franchisee results; 1,006 accounts, 23.1% margin after disclosed (incomplete) expenses

veteranDiscount.note: Veteran-targeted listings exist (syncrevenue.com) but a specific discount amount was not verified

multiUnitAvailable.note: FDD fee structure explicitly contemplates multiple contiguous territories

exclusiveTerritories.note: Territory protection based on population density/dwelling counts; franchisor reserves national-account rights inside territories

ownerTypes.note: Ownership-mode detail not explicitly disclosed in reviewed sources

trainingWeeks.note: Mandatory initial training program (Canopy System, agronomics, marketing, sales, IT, safety) — duration not disclosed

value: brand fund programs (1% of gross revenues)
required local advertising program (min $30,000/year first territory)

yearFounded.note: Founded in Raleigh, NC; affiliate flagship unit opened 2016 per Item 19

yearFranchising.note: CONFLICT: franchisepayback says franchising since 2020, but Item 20 shows 0 franchised units through 2023; effective franchise launch under Empower Brands was 2023-2024

closureCount.note: No franchise closures in Item 20 (2022-2024); system too new for meaningful attrition data

brands.notes: Lawn treatment franchise (Raleigh, NC origin) relaunched as a franchise under Empower Brands with explosive 2024 unit sales (37 openings in one year) but zero seasoned franchisees. Item 19 is a single affiliate unit with materially excluded expenses. Total cost of ownership is higher than headline fees suggest due to $30k minimum local ad spend and ramping minimum royalties.

brands.name: Daisy

riskFlags.title: Very young, VC-backed system
riskFlags.detail: Founded 2023, franchising since January 2024, ~10 units. Raised a $15M Series C in March 2025 to accelerate growth. No multi-year franchisee performance history exists; growth-stage investor pressure may prioritize expansion speed.

riskFlags.title: No exclusive territory
riskFlags.detail: Per FDD-derived sources, Daisy does not grant exclusive territories and retains the right to operate competing locations, authorize other franchisees, and use alternative sales channels within a franchisee's assigned region.

riskFlags.title: Widely conflicting third-party figures — verify against current FDD
riskFlags.detail: Investment ranges reported as $36,500-$308,500 (Entrepreneur), $210,000-$309,000 (franchisepayback/sharpsheets), and $236,550-$954,100 (Vetted Biz, whose Daisy page also contained corrupted data referencing an unrelated gym brand). Revenue figures of $4.5M-$6.2M circulate without verifiable Item 19 sourcing. The spread partly reflects Daisy's conversion model (acquiring/converting existing AV-integration businesses) vs greenfield starts.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur vs FranchisePayback vs Vetted Biz

liquidCapitalMin.note: Entrepreneur lists $75,000-$150,000 liquid capital; Vetted Biz lists $85,000

totalInvestmentMin.note: CONFLICT: 2024 FDD-based sources list $210,000 min; low end likely reflects conversion of an existing integration business

totalInvestmentMax.note: Franchisepayback lists $309,000; Vetted Biz lists up to $954,100 (data quality concerns on that page)

franchiseFee.note: Entrepreneur shows $25,000-$50,000 range (lower tier likely for conversions)

adFundPercent.note: CONFLICT: franchisepayback states 8%; 3% corroborated by sharpsheets-derived search results

avgUnitRevenue.note: Third-party figures of $6,220,000 (franchisepayback, marked 'undisclosed') and $4,539,007 (Vetted Biz, corrupted page) could not be verified as genuine Item 19 disclosures — excluded

exclusiveTerritories.note: FDD explicitly grants no exclusive territory; franchisor retains competing channels

ownerTypes.note: Absentee and part-time not permitted; brand targets existing smart-home/AV integration business owners for conversion

trainingWeeks.note: Mandatory 5-10 day initial program (Newport Beach/Costa Mesa, CA or virtual); Entrepreneur lists 40 hrs on-the-job + 40 hrs classroom

value: additional training available (extra fees may apply)
brand/ad fund programs (3% of gross sales)

totalUnits.note: Directory counts lag; Daisy has been acquiring and converting existing integrators through 2025, so the operating footprint may be larger

yearFounded.note: Founded by CEO Hagan Kappler; parent Daisyco Inc., Costa Mesa, CA

closureCount.note: System too new for closure history

isGrowing.note: 0 to ~10+ units since Jan 2024; $15M Series C (Mar 2025) earmarked for franchise growth

brands.notes: Smart-home/small-business technology installation and service franchise (Costa Mesa, CA). Legitimate, fast-moving, VC-backed startup franchise with a conversion-heavy model (acquiring existing AV integrators), which explains the wild variance in published investment figures. 10% royalty + 3% ad fund and no territorial exclusivity. The Vetted Biz profile for Daisy contained cross-contaminated data from an unrelated brand and was discounted.

brands.name: CR3 American Exteriors

riskFlags.title: Small, young system with no Item 19
riskFlags.detail: Approximately 10 total units (7 franchised + 3 company-owned, 2024 FDD), franchising only since 2022/2023, and no financial performance representation disclosed (AUV listed as undisclosed). Unit economics cannot be validated from public data.

riskFlags.title: Inconsistent figures across directories
riskFlags.detail: Franchise fee reported as $35,000 (Franchise Gator/Franchise Direct) vs $40,000 (franchisepayback, FDD-based); royalty as 6% (FDD-based) vs 6.5% (Gator); franchising since 2022 (payback) vs 2023 (Gator). Gator also lists liquid capital $75,000 against net worth $50,000, which is internally inconsistent.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Gator simultaneously lists net worth $50,000, which is inconsistent; treat with caution

franchiseFee.note: CONFLICT: Franchise Gator and Franchise Direct list $35,000

adFundPercent.note: 1% of prior month's gross revenue to system marketing fund

veteranDiscount.note: 10% off franchise fee for qualified veterans

exclusiveTerritories.note: Protected territory; franchisor may serve national accounts extending into franchise areas

ownerTypes.note: Ownership-mode detail not explicitly disclosed in reviewed sources

trainingWeeks.note: In-person training in Pennsylvania plus online modules; duration not disclosed

value: system-wide marketing fund (1% of gross revenue)

totalUnits.note: 7 franchised + 3 company-owned (2024 FDD); Gator lists 11 total

isGrowing.note: 0 to 7 franchised units since franchising began; growth positive but from a tiny base

brands.notes: Roofing/exteriors (roofing, gutters, siding, windows/doors) franchise headquartered in Pennsylvania, serving residential and commercial customers. Genuine emerging franchise with FDD on file, but tiny system, no Item 19, and modest cross-source inconsistencies in fee/royalty/timeline figures. Third-party financing available.
